Off Calendar Aug-31-2022 Continued To Oct-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Byram, for Jeremy Levin, Jonathan Levin, and Elise Patler, Petitioners; Oliver Bray, via Zoom, for Mark Unger, Trustee.

The court advises that it is not prepared to grant the relief being sought as the court has no idea of what the terms of the settlement are and is unwilling to bind the court to CCP 664.4. Ms. Bray reports that one of the terms of the agreement calls for a request that the court deny the petitions with prejudice but that her clients are not opposed to filing a Notice of Dismissal instead. Mr. Bray acknowledges the court's position and reports of the delays in resolving this matter and suggests that the court issue an Order to Show Cause for a future date and see if anyone appears and hopefully resolve the matter then. Ms. Byram agrees with Mr. Bray's suggestion.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Oct-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court advises that it will be issuing an Order to Show Cause for the same date.

Appearances:
Jennifer Byram for Sheldon Levin, MD., Petitioner.
Peter Meyers for and with Alan Abelson, Beneficiary/Successor Trustee.
Lily Harris for Alan Abelson, Objector, via Court Call.
Christopher Heck for David Golden, Objector, via Court Call.

Mr. Meyers stated the matter is not ready for trial and it needs more discovery. Ms. Byram stated it has been 22 months from date of death, the first petition was filed in April 2018, there had been time for discovery, there are issues with parties dying, her client is 97 years old, and request the court to not allow this to drag out for another year. The Court inquired if there are certain issues that can be tried/mediated first, i.e. deal with validity of trust first or petition for professional fiduciary. Mr. Meyers stated parties are unable to agree on substantive stuff on the joint status statement and they could do case management discussion, that his client is the named successor trustee who is trying to move things along by interviewing the drafting attorney and is in favor of marshalling all assets. Ms. Byram stated there is no money in the trust, that the only asset is a condo, that Mr. Abelson has changed the locks to the condo, refused to give her client the key and/or alarm code and will not allow access unless personally escorted by Mr. Abelson.

All five petitions are continued to November 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Parties are to file a joint status conference statement with any issues the court can decide on without discovery, i.e. appointing a neutral fiduciary, by November 12, 2019.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Counsel reported that they are actively engaged in discovery (document demands/depositions), that the matter is not ready for trial setting, that a supplement to schedule A and a supplement to the petition for order confirming equitable interest in property, etc. were submitted electronically for filing on April 2nd, that courtesy copies of the objections and courtesy copies of the petition to determine validity of purported will, etc. were delivered to the Court on March 29th, that the Trustee is using his own funds (about $1,000.00 a month) to cover expenses, that they do not have all of the addresses for the contingent beneficiaries, etc. Counsel also spoke of the various bank accounts, the pay on death accounts, whether or not the heggstad petition is ready to be ruled on, the need to obtain an order from the Court to determine whether or not the treasury direct account exist, the condominium and the tangible personal property being the only things titled in the trust, the Trustee being allowed to marshal some of the assets for the purpose of addressing expenses, the Trustee having sole authority to serve in the event probate is needed, the Court issuing a bifurcated order with respect to some of the bank accounts, etc. The Petition to Determine Invalidity of Purported Will, etc., the Petition to Determine Title to Property Held in Name of Decedent, etc., and the Petition for Order Confirming Equitable Interest in Property Titled in the Trust, etc. are continued to May 1, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
